plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and meet mandate.
Sell SOXL and SPAL entirely to eliminate all leveraged ETF exposure.
Increase QQQI to 25% to favor income and broad index exposure.
Buy CRM at 10% as a top-quality, high-scoring long setup.
Establish a 15% cash buffer for capital preservation and stability.
conservative.read: This conservative plan aims for a target of ~1.5%/month, prioritizing capital preservation. Risk is significantly reduced by eliminating leveraged ETFs and maintaining a substantial cash buffer, leading to lower expected drawdown.

moves.note: Reduce leveraged ETF to small size

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the concentration mandate.
Sell SOXL due to its poor performance and trim SPAL to 2% to reduce leverage.
Trim QQQI to 15% as it was flagged for trimming.
Buy ZS and META at 10% each, adding quality tech setups.
Maintain a 10% cash buffer for flexibility and risk management.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets ~2.5%/month, focusing on risk-first growth. It reduces leverage and concentration while blending broad index with quality tech, aiming for moderate growth with controlled drawdown.

moves.note: Add leveraged ETF for tech exposure

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the concentration mandate.
Sell SOXL due to its significant underperformance and below-stop status.
Trim QQQI to 10% as it was flagged for trimming.
Add CRWD to 20% to press an existing uptrending tech holding.
Buy NVDU at 10% and TECS at 5% to add high-scoring leveraged tech/semiconductor exposure.
Maintain a 5% cash buffer, leaning into market exposure.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets ~4%/month, aiming for stretched growth. It utilizes leveraged ETFs and concentrates in high-scoring setups. This approach significantly increases both potential upside and drawdown, with leveraged ETFs subject to daily decay and rebalancing, requiring strict stop discipline.

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and enhance stability.
Sell SOXL and SPAL entirely to eliminate all leveraged ETF exposure.
Increase QQQI to 30% to prioritize steady monthly distributions.
Trim QQQ to 10% to reduce exposure to underperforming broad index.
Buy XLE at 10% for sector diversification and potential yield.
Maintain a 15% cash buffer to prioritize low drawdown and reliability.
income.read: This income plan targets ~1%/month, focusing on parking cash for yield. It prioritizes low drawdown and reliable income through income/covered-call ETFs and broad index exposure. Distributions are variable and NAV can still fall in a selloff, so this is not a savings account.
